model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase model capacity in the micro-resnet while keeping the architecture simple and stable under the fixed 256-step budget. Wider channels and a slightly larger classifier head should reduce underfitting and improve validation loss.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With only 256 training steps, the current Adam setup is likely under-updating; increasing the learning rate should improve early optimization progress and reduce validation loss within the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With the larger model and higher learning rate, switching to AdamW can improve optimization stability and generalization by decoupling weight decay from adaptive moments under the short fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"AdamW regressed validation loss in this setup, so reverting to Adam restores the better optimizer configuration while keeping the strong gains from width and learning-rate tuning.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The model appears optimization-limited under 256 steps; a moderate LR increase from 1e-3 to 1.5e-3 may improve convergence speed without the instability risk of a large jump.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100","old":"    total_steps = steps_per_epoch * 100","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Enable a scheduler that is aligned with the actual fixed-step budget so the learning rate decays meaningfully within 256 steps, which can improve end-of-budget validation loss at the current higher base LR.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    total_steps = steps_per_epoch * 100","old":"    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The fixed-budget cosine schedule hurt performance, so revert to the no-scheduler baseline that produced the current best loss with LR 1.5e-3.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under a fixed 256-step budget, increasing batch size doubles unique samples seen during training and can improve validation loss if optimization remains stable at the current learning rate.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Given the strong gain from increasing batch size to 128 under fixed steps, testing 256 is a direct continuation that may further improve sample coverage and validation loss within the same step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch size 256 regressed validation loss and increased runtime, so revert to 128 which currently gives the best verified loss.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With short-budget training and larger effective sample coverage from batch size 128, slightly reducing weight decay may improve fit and lower validation loss without adding complexity.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After improving regularization, a slightly lower learning rate can trade a bit of speed for better stability and final fit within the same fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Lowering the learning rate hurt performance, so revert to 1.5e-3, which remains the best-performing value with the current batch size and weight decay.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The model has continued improving with stronger optimization and larger effective batch; increasing backbone width is a direct next capacity step that can further reduce underfitting within the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Capacity scaling has produced the largest gains so far; increasing width from 24 to 32 is the most direct high-upside next test under the fixed-step regime.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Width 32 regressed compared with width 24, so revert to 24 to recover the best-performing capacity setting.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With a wider backbone, increasing classifier hidden size can reduce head bottleneck and better use extracted features, potentially improving validation loss.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increasing the classifier width caused a large regression, so revert to FC_HIDDEN=64 to restore the best-performing model configuration.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.7e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"At the current best architecture and regularization, a small LR increase may improve convergence speed within 256 steps and further reduce validation loss.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.7e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"A final small learning-rate increase is the highest-upside low-complexity move after the last LR bump improved loss, and may extract a bit more progress within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}
